content_en:
Related from him as well, he said, “Do you know what Ghassaaq is?” They said, “No.” He said, “A spring in Hell, to which flows the sting of everything that possesses a sting, in terms of a snake, a scorpion, or anything else. Then it becomes foul. A human is brought to it and is dipped one time into it. He will then come out and his skin will have fallen from the bones. His skin and flesh, will hang from his ankles, and he will drag his flesh just as a man drags his garment.”

Those Ahaadeeth Which Mention Its Names: Which Of Them Are Authentic And Which Of Them Are Otherwise?

BL Ah x Pe a La a CS EG 9 ayy CAL & Up

546 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

“But as for him whose balance (of good deeds) will be light, He will

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 547

world). Then he will carry it and place it on his shoulder, Then he will climb with it in the Fire of Hell, until he sees that he has come out, at which point he will slip and it (the trust) will fall. He will fall after it forever. The Amannah (the trust) is in the prayer; the Amaanah is in the Sawm (fasting); the Amaanah is in Al-Wudoo (ablution); and the Amaanah is in Al-hadith (speaking and perhaps this is also referring to relating hadith). And more severe than all of that is something which one was entrusted with to keep (and then to return later on to its proprietor).” Zathaan said, “I met Al-Baraa and said, ‘Do you not hear what your brother, ‘Abdullah, says?’ He said, “He has spoken the truth.” (Related by Al-Haafidh Ad-Diyaa) This hadith is related neither in Al-Musnad nor in the “Six Books.”

A Prison In Hell That Is Called Boolus ~ May Allah ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him Belongs Might And Majesty) Protect Us From

We have hitherto mentioned this prison in a hadith related by Imam Ahmad and narrated by ‘Amr ibn Shuaib from his father from his grandfather from the Prophet #8.

The Well Of Grief

Abu Hurairah # related that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “Seek refuge with Allah from the Well of Grief.” The Companions said, “O Messenger of Allah, and what is the Well of Grief?” He sg said, “A valley in Hell, which Hell itself seeks refuge from 400 times every day. It was prepared for the reciters (of the Quran) who do their deeds for display, and the most detested of Qurraa (reciters) to Allah are those who do deeds to show them off to oppressive rulers.” (Related by ‘Alee ibn Harb) At-Tirmidhee and ibn Maajah related it from the hadith of ‘Ammaar ibn Saif from Abu Mu’aadh, and that is what is correct. At-Tirmidhee summarized it and said, “It is Ghareeb.’” And in his narration the wording is, “100 times.” Tbn Maajah mentioned a longer version, and with him also is the phrasing, “Those who do deeds to show them off to oppressive rulers.”

548, Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations

A River In Hell Which Is Like The Filth, Waste, And Putrid Material In The World ~ We Ask Allah % To Protect Us From It By His Grace And Favor

One Who Is Addicted To Alcohol, One Who Breaks Off Ties With Relations, And One Who Believes In Magic: None Of These Three Enters Paradise

Abu Moosa related that the Prophet # said, “Three will not enter Paradise: the one who is addicted to alcohol, the one who breaks off ties with relations, and the one who believes in magic. And whosoever dies, addicted to alcohol, Allah will make him drink from the river of Al-Ghootah.” It was said, “And what is the river of Al-Ghootah?” He #8 said, “A river which flows from the private parts of female fornicators. The people of the Hellfire are harmed by the smell of their private parts.” (Almad)

The Valley Of Lamlam

Abu Hurairah related that the Messenger of Allah ## said, “Verily, in Hell there is a valley that is called Lamlam. And indeed, the valleys of Hell seek refuge in Allah from its heat.” (Related by Al-Hasan ibn Sufyaan) This hadith is Ghareeb.

A Valley With A Well In Hell; The Valley Is Called Habhab

Muhammad ibn Waasai’ said, “I entered upon Bilal ibn Abee Burdah, and I said to him: O Bilal, indeed your father related to me from his father from the Prophet #, that he #8 said, ‘Indeed in Hell there is a valley called Habhab; it is a right upon Allah that every arrogant tyrant live in it, so beware, so-and-so, from being among, those who live in it” (Related by Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) At-Tabaraanee related the same from Muhammad ibn Waasai’. In his narration, Muhammad ibn Waasai’ entered upon Bilal dbn Abee Burdah ibn Abee Moosa, and said to him, “Indeed your father related to me from your grandfather & that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, ‘Indeed in Hell there is a valley in which there is a well. It is called Habhab. It is a right upon Allah that every arrogant tyrant

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 549

live in it.” In this narration, Azhar ibn Sinaan was the sole narrator at his level of the chain, and some of the Huffaaz have spoken about him, mentioning him to be soft (ie. not strong in his narrations).

The Mention Of Wail And Sa’ood

The Meaning Of Al-Wail Allah 8 said:

Be oe Boe SU 3S BY

“Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of Resurrection)" And Allah $% said:

“! will oblige him to (climb a slippery mountain in the Hellfire

called As-Sa’ood, or to) face a severe torment’ 3 Abu Sa’eed s related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “Wail is a valley in Hell, the disbelievers fall into it for 40 autumns before they reach its floor. And As-Sa’ood is a mountain in the Hellfire. The (disbeliever) will climb it for 70 autumns and will then fall in it for the same tirne — he is in it forever.” (Ahmad) At-Tirmidhee related it as well, but through a different chain, He then said, “Ghareeb, for we do not know it except by way of Ibn Luhai‘ah.”” But Tbn Jareer did relate it without going through Ibn Luhai/ah. At any rate, the hadith is Ghareeb; rather, it is Munkar. What appears stronger in the Tafseer of “Wail” is that it is the opposite of safety, healthiness, and deliverance.

The Meaning Of Sa’ood

Abu Sa’eed # related that, regarding the Verse which mentions AsSa’ood (Quran 74:17), the Messenger of Allah 2 said, “Tt is a mountain in Hell. The disbeliever is charged with climbing it. When he places his hand on it, his hand melts. When he raises it, it returns.

550 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

And when he places his leg on it, his leg melts; and when he raises it, it returns.” Al-Bazzaar, ibn Jareer, ibn Abee Haatim, and ibn Mirdawai all related it.

Qataadah related that ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Sa’eod is a rock in Hell. Upon it the disbeliever is dragged on his face.” As-Siddee said, “Sa‘ood is a smooth rock in Hell, and the disbeliever is charged with climbing it” Regarding the above-mentioned Verse (ie. Quran 74:17), Mujaahid said, “This means a difficult punishment.” And Qataadah said, “A punishment from which there is no rest.” ibn Jareer chose this last opinion.

Its Snakes And Its Lizards - May Allah Protect Us From It And From Them

content_id:
Diriwayatkan pula darinya, ia berkata, "Tahukah kalian apa itu Ghassaq?" Mereka berkata, "Tidak." Ia berkata, "Sebuah mata air di neraka, ke mana mengalir sengatan segala sesuatu yang memiliki sengatan, berupa ular, kalajengking, atau hal lainnya. Kemudian ia menjadi busuk. Seorang manusia dibawa kepadanya dan dicelupkan sekali ke dalamnya. Ia kemudian keluar dan kulitnya telah jatuh dari tulang-tulangnya. Kulit dan dagingnya akan tergantung di pergelangan kakinya, dan ia akan menyeret dagingnya seperti seorang laki-laki menyeret pakaiannya."

Hadith-Hadith Yang Menyebutkan Nama-Namanya: Mana Yang Shahih Dan Mana Yang Tidak?

546 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

"Dan adapun orang-orang yang ringan timbangan (amal kebaikan)nya, maka tempat kembalinya adalah Hawiyah (lubang, yaitu neraka)."

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 547

dunia). Kemudian ia akan membawanya dan meletakkannya di bahunya. Kemudian ia akan mendaki dengannya di neraka Jahannam, hingga ia melihat bahwa ia telah keluar, pada saat itu ia akan terpeleset dan ia (amanat) akan jatuh. Ia akan jatuh mengikutinya selamanya. Amaanah (amanat) ada dalam shalat; Amaanah ada dalam Saum (puasa); Amaanah ada dalam Al-Wudhu (wudu); dan Amaanah ada dalam Al-hadith (berbicara dan mungkin ini juga merujuk pada periwayatan hadith). Dan yang lebih berat dari semua itu adalah sesuatu yang dipercayakan kepada seseorang untuk dijaga (dan kemudian dikembalikan kepada pemiliknya). Zadhaan berkata, "Aku bertemu Al-Baraa dan berkata, 'Tidakkah engkau mendengar apa yang dikatakan saudaramu, 'Abdullah?' Ia berkata, 'Ia telah berkata benar.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Haafidh Ad-Diyaa) Hadith ini tidak terdapat dalam Al-Musnad maupun dalam "Enam Kitab."

Sebuah Penjara Di Neraka Yang Disebut Boolus — Semoga Allah 'Azza wa Jall (Bagi-Nya Segala Kemuliaan dan Keagungan) Melindungi Kita Darinya

Kami telah sebelumnya menyebutkan penjara ini dalam sebuah hadith yang diriwayatkan oleh Imam Ahmad dan diceritakan oleh 'Amr ibn Shu'aib dari ayahnya dari kakeknya dari Nabi #.

Sumur Duka Cita

Abu Hurairah # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Berlindunglah kepada Allah dari Sumur Duka Cita." Para Sahabat berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, dan apakah Sumur Duka Cita itu?" Beliau # bersabda, "Sebuah lembah di neraka Jahannam, yang dari neraka Jahannam itu sendiri berlindung 400 kali setiap hari. Ia disiapkan bagi para pembaca (Al-Quran) yang melakukan perbuatan mereka untuk pamer, dan yang paling dibenci dari para Qurraa (pembaca) kepada Allah adalah mereka yang melakukan perbuatan untuk memperlihatkannya kepada penguasa-penguasa yang zalim." (Diriwayatkan oleh 'Ali ibn Harb) At-Tirmidhee dan Ibn Maajah meriwayatkannya dari hadith 'Ammaar ibn Saif dari Abu Mu'aadh, dan itulah yang benar. At-Tirmidhee meringkasnya dan berkata, "Ini Ghareeb." Dan dalam narasi beliau redaksinya adalah "100 kali." Ibn Maajah menyebutkan versi yang lebih panjang, dan bersamanya juga terdapat ungkapan, "Mereka yang melakukan perbuatan untuk memperlihatkannya kepada penguasa-penguasa yang zalim."

548 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Sebuah Sungai Di Neraka Yang Seperti Kotoran, Limbah, Dan Materi Busuk Di Dunia — Kami Memohon Kepada Allah # Untuk Melindungi Kita Darinya Dengan Karunia Dan Kemurahan-Nya

Orang Yang Kecanduan Minuman Keras, Orang Yang Memutuskan Tali Silaturahmi, Dan Orang Yang Mempercayai Sihir: Tidak Ada Seorang Pun Dari Ketiga Orang Ini Yang Masuk Surga

Abu Moosa meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi # bersabda, "Tiga orang tidak akan masuk surga: orang yang kecanduan minuman keras, orang yang memutuskan tali silaturahmi, dan orang yang mempercayai sihir. Dan barang siapa meninggal dunia dalam keadaan kecanduan minuman keras, Allah akan memberinya minum dari sungai Al-Ghootah." Dikatakan, "Dan apakah sungai Al-Ghootah itu?" Beliau # bersabda, "Sebuah sungai yang mengalir dari bagian kemaluan para wanita pezina. Orang-orang neraka Jahannam tersiksa oleh bau bagian kemaluan mereka." (Ahmad)

Lembah Lamlam

Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Sesungguhnya di neraka Jahannam terdapat sebuah lembah yang disebut Lamlam. Dan sesungguhnya, lembah-lembah neraka berlindung kepada Allah dari panasnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Hasan ibn Sufyaan) Hadith ini Ghareeb.

Sebuah Lembah Dengan Sebuah Sumur Di Neraka; Lembah Itu Disebut Habhab

Muhammad ibn Waasi' berkata, "Aku masuk menemui Bilal ibn Abee Burdah, dan aku berkata kepadanya: Wahai Bilal, sesungguhnya ayahmu meriwayatkan kepadaku dari ayahnya dari Nabi #, bahwa beliau # bersabda, 'Sesungguhnya di neraka Jahannam terdapat sebuah lembah yang disebut Habhab; adalah hak Allah bahwa setiap penguasa lalim yang sombong tinggal di sana, maka berhati-hatilah, wahai fulan, dari termasuk orang-orang yang tinggal di sana.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) At-Tabaraanee meriwayatkan hal yang sama dari Muhammad ibn Waasi'. Dalam narasi beliau, Muhammad ibn Waasi' masuk menemui Bilal ibn Abee Burdah ibn Abee Moosa, dan berkata kepadanya, "Sesungguhnya ayahmu meriwayatkan kepadaku dari kakekmu # bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, 'Sesungguhnya di neraka Jahannam terdapat sebuah lembah yang di dalamnya terdapat sebuah sumur. Ia disebut Habhab. Adalah hak Allah bahwa setiap penguasa lalim yang sombong tinggal di sana.'"

Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar 549

Dalam narasi ini, Azhar ibn Sinaan adalah satu-satunya perawi pada tingkatnya dalam sanad, dan sebagian dari para Huffazh telah berbicara tentangnya, menyebutnya lemah (yakni tidak kuat dalam periwayatannya).

Penyebutan Wail Dan Sa'ood

Makna Al-Wail

Allah # berfirman: "Kecelakaan yang besarlah pada hari itu bagi orang-orang yang mendustakan (hari kiamat)!" Dan Allah # berfirman: "Aku akan membebaninya mendaki pendakian yang memayahkan (atau menyiksanya dengan azab yang berat)." Abu Sa'eed # meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah # bersabda, "Wail adalah sebuah lembah di neraka Jahannam, orang-orang kafir jatuh ke dalamnya selama 40 musim gugur sebelum mencapai dasarnya. Dan As-Sa'ood adalah sebuah gunung di neraka Jahannam. (Orang kafir) mendakinya selama 70 musim gugur dan kemudian jatuh di dalamnya untuk waktu yang sama — ia berada di dalamnya selamanya." (Ahmad) At-Tirmidhee juga meriwayatkannya, tetapi melalui sanad yang berbeda. Ia kemudian berkata, "Ghareeb, karena kami tidak mengetahuinya kecuali melalui jalan Ibn Luhai'ah." Tetapi Ibn Jareer meriwayatkannya tanpa melalui Ibn Luhai'ah. Bagaimanapun, hadith ini Ghareeb; bahkan, ia Munkar. Apa yang tampak lebih kuat dalam penafsiran "Wail" adalah bahwa itu adalah kebalikan dari keselamatan, kesehatan, dan kelepasan.

Makna Sa'ood

Abu Sa'eed # meriwayatkan bahwa, berkenaan dengan Ayat yang menyebutkan As-Sa'ood (Quran 74:17), Rasulullah # bersabda, "Itu adalah sebuah gunung di neraka Jahannam. Orang kafir dibebani untuk mendakinya. Ketika ia meletakkan tangannya di atasnya, tangannya meleleh. Ketika ia mengangkatnya, ia kembali lagi.

550 Kitab Akhir Zaman: Fitnah dan Cobaan Besar

Dan ketika ia meletakkan kakinya di atasnya, kakinya meleleh; dan ketika ia mengangkatnya, ia kembali lagi." Al-Bazzaar, Ibn Jareer, Ibn Abee Haatim, dan Ibn Mirdawai semuanya meriwayatkannya.

Qataadah meriwayatkan bahwa Ibn 'Abbaas berkata, "Sa'ood adalah sebuah batu di neraka Jahannam. Di atasnya orang kafir diseret dengan wajahnya." As-Siddee berkata, "Sa'ood adalah sebuah batu licin di neraka Jahannam, dan orang kafir dibebani untuk mendakinya." Berkenaan dengan Ayat yang disebutkan di atas (yaitu Quran 74:17), Mujaahid berkata, "Ini berarti hukuman yang berat." Dan Qataadah berkata, "Hukuman yang tidak ada istirahatnya." Ibn Jareer memilih pendapat terakhir ini.

Ular-Ular Dan Kadal-Kadal Neraka — Semoga Allah Melindungi Kita Darinya

"Dan janganlah orang-orang yang bakhil dengan apa yang Allah berikan kepada mereka dari karunia-Nya menyangka bahwa kebakhilan itu baik bagi mereka (sehingga mereka tidak membayar zakat yang wajib). Sebenarnya kebakhilan itu lebih buruk..."
content_ar: Related from him as well, قال, “Do you know what Ghassaaq is?” قالوا, “No.” قال, “A spring in النار, to which flows the sting of everything that possesses a sting, in terms of a snake, a scorpion, or anything else. Then it becomes foul. A human is brought to it and is dipped one time into it. He will then come out and his skin will have fallen from the bones. His skin and flesh, will hang from his ankles, and he will drag his flesh just as a man drags his garment.” Those Ahaadeeth Which Mention Its Names: Which Of Them Are Authentic And Which Of Them Are Otherwise? Then he will carry it and place it on his shoulder, Then he will climb with it in the Fire of النار, حتى he sees that he has come out, at which point he will slip and it (the trust) will fall. He will fall بعد it forever. The Amannah (the trust) is in the الصلاة; the Amaanah is in the الصيام (الصيام); the Amaanah is in Al-الوضوء (ablution); and the Amaanah is in Al-الحديث (speaking and perhaps this is أيضاً referring to relating الحديث). And more severe than all of that is something which one was entrusted with to keep (and then to return later on to its proprietor).” Zathaan said, “I met Al-Baraa and said, ‘Do you not hear what your الأخ, ‘Abdullah, says?’ قال, “He has spoken the الحق.” (Related by Al-Haafidh Ad-Diyaa) This الحديث is related neither in Al-Musnad nor in the “Six الكتب.” A Prison In النار That Is Called Boolus ~ May الله ‘Azza Wa-Jall (To Him Belongs Might And Majesty) Protect Us From We have hitherto mentioned this prison in a الحديث related by الإمام أحمد and narrated by ‘Amr ibn Shuaib from his الأب from his grandfather from النبي ﷺ #8. The Well Of الحزن أبو هريرة # related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, “Seek refuge with الله from the Well of الحزن.” The الصحابة said, “O Messenger of الله, and what is the Well of الحزن?” He sg said, “A valley in النار, which النار itself seeks refuge from 400 times every day. It was prepared for the reciters (of the القرآن الكريم) who do their deeds for display, and the most detested of Qurraa (reciters) to الله are those who do deeds to show them off to oppressive rulers.” (Related by ‘Alee ibn Harb) At-Tirmidhee and ibn Maajah related it from the الحديث of ‘Ammaar ibn Saif from Abu Mu’aadh, and that is what is correct. At-Tirmidhee summarized it and said, “It is Ghareeb.’” And in his narration the wording is, “100 times.” Tbn Maajah mentioned a longer version, and with him أيضاً is the phrasing, “Those who do deeds to show them off to oppressive rulers.” 548, الكتاب of The End : Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations A River In النار Which Is Like The Filth, Waste, And Putrid Material In The الدنيا ~ We Ask الله % To Protect Us From It By His الفضل And النعمة One Who Is Addicted To Alcohol, One Who Breaks Off Ties With Relations, And One Who Believes In Magic: None Of These Three Enters الجنة Abu Moosa related that النبي ﷺ # said, “Three will not enter الجنة: the one who is addicted to alcohol, the one who breaks off ties with relations, and the one who believes in magic. And whosoever dies, addicted to alcohol, الله will make him drink from the river of Al-Ghootah.” It was said, “And what is the river of Al-Ghootah?” He #8 said, “A river which flows from the private parts of female fornicators. The people of the النار are harmed by the smell of their private parts.” (Almad) The Valley Of Lamlam أبو هريرة related that الرسول ﷺ of الله ## said, “Verily, in النار there is a valley that is called Lamlam. And indeed, the valleys of النار seek refuge in الله from its heat.” (Related by Al-حسن ibn Sufyaan) This الحديث is Ghareeb. A Valley With A Well In النار; The Valley Is Called Habhab محمد ﷺ ibn Waasai’ said, “I entered upon Bilal ibn Abee Burdah, and I said to him: O Bilal, indeed your الأب related to me from his الأب from النبي ﷺ #, that he #8 said, ‘Indeed in النار there is a valley called Habhab; it is a الحق upon الله that every arrogant tyrant live in it, لذا beware, لذا-and-لذا, from being among, those who live in it” (Related by أبو بكر ibn Abee Ad-Dunyaa) At-Tabaraanee related the same from محمد ﷺ ibn Waasai’. In his narration, محمد ﷺ ibn Waasai’ entered upon Bilal dbn Abee Burdah ibn Abee Moosa, and said to him, “Indeed your الأب related to me from your grandfather & that الرسول ﷺ of الله #8 said, ‘Indeed in النار there is a valley in which there is a well. It is called Habhab. It is a الحق upon الله that every arrogant tyrant الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations 549 live in it.” In this narration, Azhar ibn Sinaan was the sole narrator at his level of the chain, and some of the Huffaaz have spoken about him, mentioning him to be soft (ie. not strong in his narrations). The Mention Of Wail And Sa’ood The Meaning Of Al-Wail الله 8 said: Be oe Boe SU 3S BY “Woe that Day to the deniers (of the Day of البعث)" And الله $% said: “! will oblige him to (climb a slippery mountain in the النار called As-Sa’ood, or to) face a severe torment’ 3 Abu Sa’eed s related that الرسول ﷺ of الله #% said, “Wail is a valley in النار, the disbelievers fall into it for 40 autumns قبل they reach its floor. And As-Sa’ood is a mountain in the النار. The (disbeliever) will climb it for 70 autumns and will then fall in it for the same tirne — he is in it forever.” (أحمد) At-Tirmidhee related it as well, but through a different chain, He then said, “Ghareeb, for we do not know it except by الطريق of Ibn Luhai‘ah.”” But Tbn Jareer did relate it without going through Ibn Luhai/ah. At any rate, the الحديث is Ghareeb; rather, it is Munkar. What appears stronger in the Tafseer of “Wail” is that it is the opposite of safety, healthiness, and deliverance. The Meaning Of Sa’ood Abu Sa’eed # related that, فيما يخص the الآية which mentions AsSa’ood (القرآن الكريم 74:17), الرسول ﷺ of الله 2 said, “Tt is a mountain in النار. The disbeliever is charged with climbing it. عندما he places his hand on it, his hand melts. عندما he raises it, it returns. 550 الكتاب of The End: Great الابتلاءات and Tribulations And عندما he places his leg on it, his leg melts; and عندما he raises it, it returns.” Al-Bazzaar, ibn Jareer, ibn Abee Haatim, and ibn Mirdawai all related it. Qataadah related that ibn ‘Abbaas said, “Sa’eod is a rock in النار. Upon it the disbeliever is dragged on his face.” As-Siddee said, “Sa‘ood is a smooth rock in النار, and the disbeliever is charged with climbing it” فيما يخص the above-mentioned الآية (ie. القرآن الكريم 74:17), Mujaahid said, “This means a difficult العقاب.” And Qataadah said, “A العقاب from which there is no rest.” ibn Jareer chose this last opinion.
